Phaseolus lunatus var. ellipticus, also known as "Phaseolaster elliptica," is a type of wild bean plant that belongs to the legume family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phaseolus lunatus var. ellipticus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried and powdered forms that can be used in capsules or as a supplement.
